subject: english
topic_title: Structure: Noun Phrase and Verb Phrase
learning_objectives: Define a noun phrase and identify its grammatical functions
Define a verb phrase and identify its components
Construct and analyse noun phrases and verb phrases in sentences

sections.heading: Noun Phrase – Definition
sections.content: A noun phrase (NP) is a phrase in which the main word (the head) is a noun or pronoun. The noun phrase performs exactly the same functions as a noun.

sections.heading: Functions of the Noun Phrase
sections.content:
(i) As subject of a sentence:
The big ugly girl in the class is quarrelsome.
A young talented Nigerian lady was presented an award last year.
Gerundial phrase as subject: Telling lies often belittles a person.
Infinitive as subject: To tell lies belittles a person.
Adjective as head: The poor suffer a lot. | The wise learn from experience.

(ii) As object of a verb:
The officer praised the students. (direct object)

(iii) As subject complement:
The grateful boy called his friend saviour.
His village made him a chief.

(iv) As complement of a preposition:
The governor thought of the brilliant lawyer when choosing his cabinet.
The thief hid the gun under his bed.

sections.heading: Verb Phrase – Definition and Structure
sections.content:
A verb phrase (VP) is made up of a main verb and one or more auxiliary verbs.

One auxiliary + main verb:
Dike has done the work.

Two auxiliaries + main verb:
Dike has been doing the work since morning.

Modal auxiliary + main verb:
Obioma may come with us if he likes.
